title: Content Codings, Character Sets, Media Admission, and Digests · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds representation decoding and identity ledger from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of representation decoding and identity ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decoded, retained, parsed, and extracted sizes and stop before resource ceilings.
Validate content-coding order and classify unsupported, corrupt, partial, over-budget, and cancellation outcomes.
Use transport media type plus conservative sniff evidence to accept HTML or XML and reject binary and ambiguous bodies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Wire Decoded Budgets
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6101 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Wire Decoded Budgets.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for track wire, compressed, decoded, retained, parsed, and extracted sizes and stop before resource ceilings. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Wire Decoded Budgets, keep Wire Decoded Budgets connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Stage Digests
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-stage-digests-1b67gc" that uses case 1299 to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
